raw_response: {"rating": 2.3, "lean": "Lean Right", "explanation": "This is a news report. The author’s framing and source selection consistently cast skepticism on NASEM’s climate-mental health findings and the EPA’s Endangerment Finding, favoring right-leaning critiques without balancing counter-perspectives, resulting in a Lean Right rating. The directional cues come from the author’s own verbs, related-link headlines, and asymmetric sourcing rather than explicit ideological argumentation. Detailed Analysis: (1) The lede frames NASEM’s point as a “claim” that experts “rebuked,” a loaded verb choice that validates critics and primes readers to doubt NASEM; (2) Source selection is overwhelmingly from right-leaning or climate-skeptic circles (Cato Institute, Heritage Foundation, Heartland Institute, former Trump EPA official) with no expert defenders of NASEM or of climate-mental health research included, an omission indicating one-sided framing; (3) The author repeatedly foregrounds what unnamed “experts” have said about the Endangerment Finding and related issues while not providing balancing viewpoints from mainstream climate or public health researchers, reinforcing a single-direction narrative; (4) Embedded related-link headlines — “Insiders Say National Academy Of Sciences’ Climate Report Clapping Back At Trump EPA Has More Holes Than Swiss Cheese” and “Dems Finally Realizing Most Americans Don’t Want Enormous Climate Agenda Imposed On Them” — are partisan framings that bolster a right-leaning stance; (5) The piece closes by highlighting a Republican-led investigation into NASEM for possible “partisan aims” without presenting NASEM’s response, further tilting skepticism toward NASEM; (6) While the article neutrally summarizes portions of NASEM’s findings, the cumulative framing and editorial choices favor a right-leaning perspective."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report. The author’s framing and source selection consistently cast skepticism on NASEM’s climate-mental health findings and the EPA’s Endangerment Finding, favoring right-leaning critiques without balancing counter-perspectives, resulting in a Lean Right rating. The directional cues come from the author’s own verbs, related-link headlines, and asymmetric sourcing rather than explicit ideological argumentation. Detailed Analysis: (1) The lede frames NASEM’s point as a “claim” that experts “rebuked,” a loaded verb choice that validates critics and primes readers to doubt NASEM; (2) Source selection is overwhelmingly from right-leaning or climate-skeptic circles (Cato Institute, Heritage Foundation, Heartland Institute, former Trump EPA official) with no expert defenders of NASEM or of climate-mental health research included, an omission indicating one-sided framing; (3) The author repeatedly foregrounds what unnamed “experts” have said about the Endangerment Finding and related issues while not providing balancing viewpoints from mainstream climate or public health researchers, reinforcing a single-direction narrative; (4) Embedded related-link headlines — “Insiders Say National Academy Of Sciences’ Climate Report Clapping Back At Trump EPA Has More Holes Than Swiss Cheese” and “Dems Finally Realizing Most Americans Don’t Want Enormous Climate Agenda Imposed On Them” — are partisan framings that bolster a right-leaning stance; (5) The piece closes by highlighting a Republican-led investigation into NASEM for possible “partisan aims” without presenting NASEM’s response, further tilting skepticism toward NASEM; (6) While the article neutrally summarizes portions of NASEM’s findings, the cumulative framing and editorial choices favor a right-leaning perspective.

explanation: This is a news report. The author’s framing and source selection consistently cast skepticism on NASEM’s climate-mental health findings and the EPA’s Endangerment Finding, favoring right-leaning critiques without balancing counter-perspectives, resulting in a Lean Right rating. The directional cues come from the author’s own verbs, related-link headlines, and asymmetric sourcing rather than explicit ideological argumentation. Detailed Analysis: (1) The lede frames NASEM’s point as a “claim” that experts “rebuked,” a loaded verb choice that validates critics and primes readers to doubt NASEM; (2) Source selection is overwhelmingly from right-leaning or climate-skeptic circles (Cato Institute, Heritage Foundation, Heartland Institute, former Trump EPA official) with no expert defenders of NASEM or of climate-mental health research included, an omission indicating one-sided framing; (3) The author repeatedly foregrounds what unnamed “experts” have said about the Endangerment Finding and related issues while not providing balancing viewpoints from mainstream climate or public health researchers, reinforcing a single-direction narrative; (4) Embedded related-link headlines — “Insiders Say National Academy Of Sciences’ Climate Report Clapping Back At Trump EPA Has More Holes Than Swiss Cheese” and “Dems Finally Realizing Most Americans Don’t Want Enormous Climate Agenda Imposed On Them” — are partisan framings that bolster a right-leaning stance; (5) The piece closes by highlighting a Republican-led investigation into NASEM for possible “partisan aims” without presenting NASEM’s response, further tilting skepticism toward NASEM; (6) While the article neutrally summarizes portions of NASEM’s findings, the cumulative framing and editorial choices favor a right-leaning perspective.
